comment: Synthetic R/R AML patient, age 62, FLT3-TKD (D835Y) primary-refractory after 7+3 induction (NO CR1 achieved — 28% blasts on day-28 BM). DIFFERENTIATION from existing patient_aml_flt3_relapse.json (which is FLT3-ITD, achieved CR1 then relapsed at 9 mo, age 55, female): this case is PRIMARY-REFRACTORY (no CR achieved), FLT3-TKD (D835Y) variant (not ITD), age 62, male, post 7+3+midostaurin induction failure. Engine routes ALGO-AML-2L step 1 (no emergency) → step 2 (no MRD — refractory not MRD-positive CR) → step 4 (RF-AML-FLT3-ACTIONABLE fires via flt3_tkd / flt3_mutation=positive; gilteritinib covers BOTH FLT3-ITD AND FLT3-TKD per ADMIRAL) → IND-AML-2L-GILTERITINIB-FLT3 (aggressive 2L+; mOS 9.3 vs 5.6 mo HR 0.64; bridge to alloHCT for fit responders).

findings.prior_regimen: 7+3 induction (cytarabine 200 mg/m² CIVI d1-7 + daunorubicin 60 mg/m² d1-3) + midostaurin 50 mg PO BID d8-21 — primary-refractory, day-28 BM blasts 28% (no CR achieved)

primary_diagnosis.disease_entity: Acute Myeloid Leukemia — FLT3-TKD (D835Y)-mutated, primary-refractory after 7+3 + midostaurin induction (no CR1 achieved)

molecular_at_relapse_or_failure.note: FLT3-TKD persists despite midostaurin exposure during induction — gilteritinib covers TKD per ADMIRAL (distinct from quizartinib which is ITD-only).

clinical_record.planned_regimen_notes: IND-AML-2L-GILTERITINIB-FLT3: gilteritinib 120 mg PO daily monotherapy per ADMIRAL (Perl 2019). Baseline + serial ECG (QTc <450 ms) + electrolyte correction (K+ ≥4.0, Mg++ ≥2.0). Differentiation-syndrome monitoring (fever, dyspnea, weight gain, pleural effusion → dexamethasone). Bridge to alloHCT for fit responders (CRITICAL — gilteritinib monotherapy NOT curative; sustained remission requires HCT). Consider post-HCT gilteritinib maintenance per emerging MORPHO data. Ukraine access: not registered — international referral / compassionate-use pathway required.
